nuggets.headline: At Nine, Pete Rango Taught Himself Guitar Due to a Tricky Teacher
nuggets.text: Pete Rango's deep musical commitment started incredibly early, picking up the guitar at just nine years old. Interestingly, he actually taught himself to play because he just couldn't quite gel with his appointed guitar teacher. That early, self-reliant approach clearly laid the groundwork for the powerful DIY spirit he embodies today, pushing him to build his own creative path as an artist and producer.

source.title: Meet Pete Rango - Voyage MIA Magazine | Miami City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: He became interested in music when he was nine years old. He taught himself how to play guitar because he couldn't get along with his teacher.

nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango in the studio, continuing his musical journey.

nuggets.headline: From Metal Bands to Live Audio for ASAP Ferg, Pete Rango's Expansive Journey
nuggets.text: As 'Oms at Play' sets its vibe, think about Pete Rango's incredible musical journey and how it shapes his sound. He went from fronting a metal/hardcore band in high school, to producing electronic and hip-hop, and even spent years freelancing as a live audio engineer for major artists like Dom Kennedy and ASAP Ferg. This broad experience—from raw punk energy to intricate electronic production and managing live sound for huge concerts—all feeds into the unique, genre-colliding musical worlds he meticulously crafts in the studio.

source.title: Meet Pete Rango - Voyage MIA Magazine | Miami City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: In high school, he started a metal/hardcore band... He has freelanced in the Audio/Visual world for over 4 years. His freelance work includes doing live audio for concerts and conferences. Concerts he has done live audio for include Dom Kennedy, DJ Mustard, ASAP Ferg...

nuggets.imageCaption: for major artists like Dom Kennedy and ASAP Ferg

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' has you grooving, check out SG Lewis's refined electronica
nuggets.text: If Pete Rango's blend of electronic and smooth rhythms on 'Oms at Play' is hitting just right, then you absolutely need to dive into SG Lewis. He's a master at crafting lush, dance-ready tracks that often feature incredible vocalists, perfectly fusing disco, house, and R&B elements into a sound that feels both classic and fresh. Start with his album *times* or his EPs like *Dark*.

source.title: rush | PETE RANGO
source.quoteSnippet: Pete Rango's work is associated with genres including electronic, experimental, hip hop, house, pop, and popindie. SG Lewis is a British musician, DJ, and record producer. He is known for his blend of electronic, disco, house and R&B music.

nuggets.imageCaption: SG Lewis blends house, disco, and R&B.

artistSummary: Pete Rango is a dynamic Colombian artist, producer, and culture-builder based in Richmond, Virginia, known for fusing electronic, hip-hop, and experimental sounds. He champions a DIY spirit, creating vibrant worlds where community, creativity, and technology converge through his work with XUE Records and L.I.V. Corp nonprofit.
